title: Guava Flow Cytometry
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"4i99i","text":"Please contact Dr. Steven Wilhelm (wilhelm@utk.ed) for additional information regarding this protocol.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Click CLEANING from the main menu Always clean the machine before and after use
Load the following: *dH 2 O in W1,W2, W3, W4, W5, W6, and position 1 *100 µL bleach in position 2 *GUAVA ICF in position 3 and 4 *dH 2 O in capillary shutdown (position 9)
To ensure optimal performance, allow the machine to warm up for at least 10 min prior to acquiring samples.
Select InCyte from the main menu
Define the worklist parameters (e.g., wells to analyze, events to acquire, time to acquire, dilution factor, original volume, replicates, etc)
Select the folder where you want to save the data file and click SAVE

Select the Analysis Method that you want to use for your specific sample set. To open a saved method, click RETRIEVE on the dialog box, then OPEN. If you want to creat a new Analysis Method, click NEW, then OK and set the parameters.
Place the microtiter plate in the tray
Load the following cleaning solutions: *dH 2 O in W2, W4, W5 *Empty tubes in W5, W6 *100 µL bleach in W1 *ICF in position 10 *dH 2 O in position 9 (capillary shutdown)
Select the sample for ADJUST SETTINGS in the dialog box
Select the type of plots and parameters you wish to display
Use the GAIN CONTROLS to select the Threshold parameter from the drop-down menu and use to adjust the gains (FSC, SSC, GRN, YLW, RED, NIR, RED2, or NIR2)
To adjust COMPENSATION, click NEXT STEP, then ADJUST SETTINGS
When you are finished adjusting the settings, click NEXT STEP.
Click RESUME WORKLIST and it will begin acquiring samples
creator: Steven W Wilhelm
